title: Using honestlm
text: honest_lm() fits regular lm() object extra class. estimates ordinary linear-model estimates, printed summary hides coefficient p-values default. categorical predictors two levels, coefficient rows comparisons reference level. separate tests whether category, whole predictor, matters. post-hoc comparisons among levels, estimated marginal means usually better tool.
code: fit <- honest_lm(mpg ~ wt + factor(cyl), data = mtcars) summary(fit) #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = mpg ~ wt + factor(cyl), data = mtcars) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -4.5890 -1.2357 -0.5159  1.3845  5.7915  #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate Std. Error t value #> (Intercept)   33.9908     1.8878  18.006 #> wt            -3.2056     0.7539  -4.252 #> factor(cyl)6  -4.2556     1.3861  -3.070 #> factor(cyl)8  -6.0709     1.6523  -3.674 #>  #> Categorical predictors: #>   factor(cyl): 3 levels; reference level = 4 #>  #> Notes: #>   * Coefficient p-values are hidden by default. Use p_values = "warn" or "show" if you really want them. #>   * For post-hoc comparisons among factor levels, consider estimated marginal means, e.g. emmeans::emmeans() and pairs(). See https://rvlenth.github.io/emmeans/. #>  #> Residual standard error: 2.557 on 28 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.8374, Adjusted R-squared:  0.82 #> F-statistic: 48.08 on 3 and 28 DF  (model-level p-value hidden) #> Warning: `factor(cyl)` has 3 levels. The coefficient rows for `factor(cyl)` are #> comparisons to the reference level `4`, not tests of whether each category or #> the overall predictor matters.

title: Using honestlm
text: Base anova() linear models reports sequential Type sums squares. honestlm leaves table intact warns interpretation.
code: anova(fit) #> Analysis of Variance Table #>  #> Response: mpg #>             Df Sum Sq Mean Sq  F value    Pr(>F)     #> wt           1 847.73  847.73 129.6650 5.079e-12 *** #> factor(cyl)  2  95.26   47.63   7.2856  0.002835 **  #> Residuals   28 183.06    6.54                        #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #> Warning: `anova()` for linear models reports sequential Type I sums of squares. #> Those depend on the order of terms in the formula and are usually not the test #> you want for models with multiple predictors. Use `car::Anova()` for Type II or #> Type III sums of squares.

title: Using honestlm
text: broom installed, tidy() removes p.value default adds contrast_note column factor contrast rows.
code: broom::tidy(fit) #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   term         estimate std.error statistic contrast_note                        #>   <chr>           <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>                                #> 1 (Intercept)     34.0      1.89      18.0  NA                                   #> 2 wt              -3.21     0.754     -4.25 NA                                   #> 3 factor(cyl)6    -4.26     1.39      -3.07 comparison to reference level `4`; … #> 4 factor(cyl)8    -6.07     1.65      -3.67 comparison to reference level `4`; …

title: Using honestlm
text: geom_lm_smooth() stat_lm_means() designed make plots match linear model structure taught.   Set interaction = TRUE want separate slopes full cell means.
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   geom_lm_smooth(interaction = FALSE) ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg, colour = factor(am))) +   geom_point() +   stat_lm_means(interaction = FALSE)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Yaniv Brandvain. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Brandvain Y (2026). honestlm: Guardrails Teaching Linear Models. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://yanivjb.github.io/honestlm/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {honestlm: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models},   author = {Yaniv Brandvain},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://yanivjb.github.io/honestlm/}, }

title: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models
text: honestlm keeps familiar linear model workflows, adds guardrails around common interpretation traps. Documentation site: https://yanivjb.github.io/honestlm/ package intentionally small. starts ordinary lm() models keeps familiar verbs like summary(), anova(), broom::tidy(), ggplot2 layers, changes defaults usual output invites overreading.

title: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models
text: Install development version GitHub: working local checkout, load package development : Run checks locally :
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("yanivjb/honestlm") devtools::load_all() devtools::test() devtools::check(document = FALSE, manual = FALSE)

title: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models
text: default, coefficient p-values hidden. categorical predictors two levels, summary() warns coefficient rows comparisons reference level, tests whether category whole predictor matters. summary also points readers toward estimated marginal means post-hoc comparisons.
code: library(honestlm) library(palmerpenguins)  fit <- honest_lm(   bill_length_mm ~ bill_depth_mm + species,   data = penguins )  summary(fit)

title: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models
text: linear models, anova() reports sequential Type sums squares. depend order terms formula. honestlm leaves table intact warns interpretation trap.
code: anova(fit)

title: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models
text: tidy() removes p.value default adds contrast_note column factor contrasts. glance() removes model-level p.value default.
code: library(broom)  tidy(fit) glance(fit)

title: Guardrails for Teaching Linear Models
text: geom_lm_smooth(interaction = FALSE) draws additive/parallel linear model smooths. Set interaction = TRUE delegate ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm") separate slopes group. stat_lm_means() draws model-predicted means rather raw grouped means. helps plots follow additive interaction structure linear model.
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(penguins, aes(bill_depth_mm, bill_length_mm, colour = species)) +   geom_point() +   geom_lm_smooth(interaction = FALSE) ggplot(penguins, aes(island, bill_length_mm, colour = species)) +   geom_point() +   stat_lm_means(interaction = FALSE) +   facet_wrap(~species)

title: Add honest linear model behavior to an existing model — as_honest_lm
code: as_honest_lm(model, p_values = NULL)

title: Add honest linear model behavior to an existing model — as_honest_lm
text: model object inheriting class "lm". p_values Optional p-value policy. See honest_lm().

title: Add honest linear model behavior to an existing model — as_honest_lm
text: object class c("honest_lm", "lm").

title: Add honest linear model behavior to an existing model — as_honest_lm
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + factor(cyl), data = mtcars) summary(as_honest_lm(fit)) #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = mpg ~ wt + factor(cyl), data = mtcars) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -4.5890 -1.2357 -0.5159  1.3845  5.7915  #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate Std. Error t value #> (Intercept)   33.9908     1.8878  18.006 #> wt            -3.2056     0.7539  -4.252 #> factor(cyl)6  -4.2556     1.3861  -3.070 #> factor(cyl)8  -6.0709     1.6523  -3.674 #>  #> Categorical predictors: #>   factor(cyl): 3 levels; reference level = 4 #>  #> Notes: #>   * Coefficient p-values are hidden by default. Use p_values = "warn" or "show" if you really want them. #>   * For post-hoc comparisons among factor levels, consider estimated marginal means, e.g. emmeans::emmeans() and pairs(). See https://rvlenth.github.io/emmeans/. #>  #> Residual standard error: 2.557 on 28 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.8374,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.82 #> F-statistic: 48.08 on 3 and 28 DF  (model-level p-value hidden) #> Warning: `factor(cyl)` has 3 levels. The coefficient rows for `factor(cyl)` are comparisons to the reference level `4`, not tests of whether each category or the overall predictor matters.

what: Linear-model smooths with additive slopes by default — geom_lm_smooth
title: Linear-model smooths with additive slopes by default — geom_lm_smooth
text: geom_lm_smooth() linear-model smoother grouped data. default, interaction = FALSE, fits one slope group offsets (y ~ x + group) inside panel. interaction = TRUE, delegates ggplot2::geom_smooth() method = "lm" ordinary separate grouped slopes.

title: Linear-model smooths with additive slopes by default — geom_lm_smooth
code: geom_lm_smooth(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   se = TRUE,   level = 0.95,   n = 100,   fullrange = FALSE,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Linear-model smooths with additive slopes by default — geom_lm_smooth
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created aes(). specified inherit.aes = TRUE (default), combined default mapping top level plot. must supply mapping plot mapping. data data displayed layer. three options: NULL, default, data inherited plot data specified call ggplot(). data.frame, object, override plot data. objects fortified produce data frame. See fortify() variables created. function called single argument, plot data. return value must data.frame, used layer data. function can created formula (e.g. ~ head(.x, 10)). ... arguments passed layer()'s params argument. arguments broadly fall one 4 categories . Notably, arguments position argument, aesthetics required can passed .... Unknown arguments part 4 categories ignored. Static aesthetics mapped scale, fixed value apply layer whole. example, colour = "red" linewidth = 3. geom's documentation Aesthetics section lists available options. 'required' aesthetics passed params. Please note passing unmapped aesthetics vectors technically possible, order required length guaranteed parallel input data. constructing layer using stat_*() function, ... argument can used pass parameters geom part layer. example stat_density(geom = "area", outline.type = ""). geom's documentation lists parameters can accept. Inversely, constructing layer using geom_*() function, ... argument can used pass parameters stat part layer. example geom_area(stat = "density", adjust = 0.5). stat's documentation lists parameters can accept. key_glyph argument layer() may also passed .... can one functions described key glyphs, change display layer legend. interaction grouped smooth use separate slopes? default FALSE draws additive/parallel linear-model smooths. se Display confidence band around smooth? (TRUE default, see level control.) level Level confidence band use (0.95 default). n Number points evaluate smoother. fullrange TRUE, smoothing line gets expanded range plot, potentially beyond data. extend line additional padding created expansion. na.rm FALSE, default, missing values removed warning. TRUE, missing values silently removed. show.legend logical. layer included legends? NA, default, includes aesthetics mapped. FALSE never includes, TRUE always includes. can also named logical vector finely select aesthetics display. include legend keys levels, even data exists, use TRUE.  NA, levels shown legend, unobserved levels omitted. inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics, rather combining . useful helper functions define data aesthetics inherit behaviour default plot specification, e.g. annotation_borders().

title: Linear-model smooths with additive slopes by default — geom_lm_smooth
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Linear-model smooths with additive slopes by default — geom_lm_smooth
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   geom_lm_smooth()

what: Fit a guarded linear model — honest_lm
title: Fit a guarded linear model — honest_lm
text: honest_lm() fits regular stats::lm() model adds honest_lm class. Familiar methods summary(), anova(), broom methods can use cautious defaults.

title: Fit a guarded linear model — honest_lm
code: honest_lm(formula, data, ..., p_values = c("hide", "warn", "show"))

title: Fit a guarded linear model — honest_lm
text: formula, data, ... Passed stats::lm(). p_values p-values handled honest methods. default "hide" suppresses p-value columns possible. "show" includes . "warn" includes may warn contrast interpretation.

title: Fit a guarded linear model — honest_lm
text: object class c("honest_lm", "lm").

title: Fit a guarded linear model — honest_lm
code: fit <- honest_lm(mpg ~ wt + factor(cyl), data = mtcars) summary(fit) #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = mpg ~ wt + factor(cyl), data = mtcars) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -4.5890 -1.2357 -0.5159  1.3845  5.7915  #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate Std. Error t value #> (Intercept)   33.9908     1.8878  18.006 #> wt            -3.2056     0.7539  -4.252 #> factor(cyl)6  -4.2556     1.3861  -3.070 #> factor(cyl)8  -6.0709     1.6523  -3.674 #>  #> Categorical predictors: #>   factor(cyl): 3 levels; reference level = 4 #>  #> Notes: #>   * Coefficient p-values are hidden by default. Use p_values = "warn" or "show" if you really want them. #>   * For post-hoc comparisons among factor levels, consider estimated marginal means, e.g. emmeans::emmeans() and pairs(). See https://rvlenth.github.io/emmeans/. #>  #> Residual standard error: 2.557 on 28 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.8374,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.82 #> F-statistic: 48.08 on 3 and 28 DF  (model-level p-value hidden) #> Warning: `factor(cyl)` has 3 levels. The coefficient rows for `factor(cyl)` are comparisons to the reference level `4`, not tests of whether each category or the overall predictor matters.

what: Model-predicted means for categorical displays — stat_lm_means
title: Model-predicted means for categorical displays — stat_lm_means
text: stat_lm_means() model-aware alternative using ggplot2::stat_summary() means intervals. draws fitted means linear model implied plot aesthetics instead raw grouped means. two categorical predictors, interaction = FALSE fits additive model interaction = TRUE fits interaction model.

title: Model-predicted means for categorical displays — stat_lm_means
code: stat_lm_means(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   geom = "pointrange",   position = "identity",   ...,   interaction = FALSE,   se = TRUE,   level = 0.95,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Model-predicted means for categorical displays — stat_lm_means
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created aes(). specified inherit.aes = TRUE (default), combined default mapping top level plot. must supply mapping plot mapping. data data displayed layer. three options: NULL, default, data inherited plot data specified call ggplot(). data.frame, object, override plot data. objects fortified produce data frame. See fortify() variables created. function called single argument, plot data. return value must data.frame, used layer data. function can created formula (e.g. ~ head(.x, 10)). geom geometric object use display data layer. using stat_*() function construct layer, geom argument can used override default coupling stats geoms. geom argument accepts following: Geom ggproto subclass, example GeomPoint. string naming geom. give geom string, strip function name geom_ prefix. example, use geom_point(), give geom "point". information ways specify geom, see layer geom documentation. position position adjustment use data layer. can used various ways, including prevent overplotting improving display. position argument accepts following: result calling position function, position_jitter(). method allows passing extra arguments position. string naming position adjustment. give position string, strip function name position_ prefix. example, use position_jitter(), give position "jitter". information ways specify position, see layer position documentation. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2 layer. interaction fitted means come interaction model? default FALSE uses additive model. se confidence intervals drawn? level Confidence level intervals. na.rm FALSE, missing values removed warning. TRUE, missing values silently removed. show.legend logical. layer included legends? NA, default, includes aesthetics mapped. FALSE never includes, TRUE always includes. can also named logical vector finely select aesthetics display. include legend keys levels, even data exists, use TRUE.  NA, levels shown legend, unobserved levels omitted. inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics, rather combining . useful helper functions define data aesthetics inherit behaviour default plot specification, e.g. annotation_borders().

title: Model-predicted means for categorical displays — stat_lm_means
text: ggplot2 layer.

title: Model-predicted means for categorical displays — stat_lm_means
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg, colour = factor(am))) +   geom_point() +   stat_lm_means()

what: Summarize a guarded linear model — summary.honest_lm
title: Summarize a guarded linear model — summary.honest_lm
text: method keeps familiar shape summary.lm() hides coefficient p-values default. model contains categorical predictor two levels, printing summary warns coefficient rows comparisons reference level, tests whether category overall predictor matters.

title: Summarize a guarded linear model — summary.honest_lm

title: Summarize a guarded linear model — summary.honest_lm
